title: Khar Delba
content:

{{Quote|This is just a decoy—all my defenses have always been on the dark side of the moon.|Naga Sadow|<ref name="flight">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 5]]</ref>}}
'''Khar Delba''' was an [[Water/Legends|icy]], snowy [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]], which was part of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] as of [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]. Khar Delba had one [[Moon/Legends|moon]], [[Khar Shian]], and was located at the edge of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Realspace/Legends|Space]]. It was the home to the [[Sith war behemoth]]. Naga Sadow placed a [[Naga Sadow's Khar Delba citadel|citadel]] there, but unbeknownst to the rest of the Sith at large, Sadow's Khar Delba citadel was a mere decoy; his [[Naga Sadow's Khar Shian citadel|real base]] was on Khar Shian. In 5000 BBY, with Naga Sadow in the midst of a power struggle with his rival, [[Ludo Kressh]], Khar Delba was [[Battle of Khar Delba|attacked]] from [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] by forces loyal to Kressh. Although the citadel was badly damaged in Kressh's [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] of the planet, Sadow emerged victorious after calling in his secret [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] from the far side of Khar Shian to counterattack Kressh. In the following [[Standard month/Legends|months]], Khar Delba was rebuilt and was established as the center of Naga Sadow's power as he gathered a force to invade the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], in a conflict that became known as the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]].

==Description==
{{Quote|Sadow must have preferred locations where sunlight never reached, for both his former properties can only be visited with an ample supply of glow rods. Those who venture to explore these dark environs may gain nothing more than a better appreciation for ancient construction methods, but I personally found the Khar Shian ruins to be extremely enlightening.|Seviss Vaa|<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>}}
[[File:Khar_Delba_surface.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The surface of Khar Delba]]
Khar Delba was located in the region of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] known as the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]] in the [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was located on the outer edge of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Realspace/Legends|Space]], which was encircled by a large [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]] called the [[Stygian Caldera]] that helped to conceal the area from outsiders. Khar Delba, along with [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]], [[Rhelg/Legends|Rhelg]], [[Krayiss II]], and [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], was one of the five sacred Sith planets linked by the looping [[Nache Bhelfia]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]]. In addition, Khar Delba was located at the endpoint of the [[Kamat Aegit]] hyperlane, which stretched to [[Begeren]] in the interior of Sith Space.<ref name="TEA" />

Khar Delba resembled an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] from space and had a [[Stone|rocky]] surface, with many [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] and craters. The planet was perpetually covered in [[Water/Legends|snow and ice]]. Khar Delba had one [[Moon/Legends|moon]], [[Khar Shian]].<ref name="fabric" /> One of the [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of Khar Delba was the fearsome [[Sith war behemoth]]. Though they were originally peaceful herd creatures, they were transformed into vicious war beasts through [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]].<ref name="behemoth" /> The planet's [[Temperature/Legends|frigid]] mountains were home to the [[Sith warbird]], a species of large, flightless [[Bird/Legends|bird]] that was used by the Sith as an [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] mount.<ref name="CSWE" />

==History==
===Early history===
{{Quote|Our work identified the looping hyperspace lanes within the Stygian Caldera that contain the planets of the Sith Empire. The Nache Bhelfia route links Ziost, Khar Delba, Rhelg, Krayiss, and Korriban—the five worlds most sacred to the Sith.|Sorzus Syn, ''Book of Sith''|<ref name="BoS" />}}
After an [[Invasion of Korriban|invasion]] by the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]] of the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]] [[Species/Legends|species]]<ref name="END:TSD">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}}</ref> and a [[Korriban civil war|civil war]]<ref name="GH07">{{TORcite|type=codex|Galactic History 07: The First Spaceflights}}</ref> on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] around [[27,700 BBY]],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> surviving members of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] species [[Colonist/Legends|colonized]] Khar Delba with Rakatan [[Starship/Legends|vessels]], [[Sith Empire expansion|integrating]] the planet into the [[Sith Empire (Pre-Republic)|ancient Sith Empire]].<ref name="BoS" /> The planet, along with the rest of Sith Space,<ref name="TEA" /> fell under the sway of 12 powerful [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]—notably including [[XoXaan]] and [[Sorzus Syn]]—who had tracked down rumors of an ancient Kingdom of the Sith and discovered the planet along the Nache Bhelfia hyperlane<ref name="bmf">{{BuildFalconCite|82|Guide to the Galaxy}}</ref> after fleeing the [[The galaxy/Legends|Galactic]] Republic after their defeat in the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]] in [[6900 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" /> Sorzus Syn later wrote of the planet in her [[Sorzus Syn's chronicle|chronicle]], which was later collected by [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] into his ''[[Book of Sith]]''.<ref name="BoS" />

===Naga Sadow's decoy===
{{Quote|You struck at the ''wrong fortress'', Ludo Kressh!|Naga Sadow|<ref name="flight" />}}
By [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]],<ref name="JvS" /> [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] claimant [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] used Khar Delba as a base and established a large [[Naga Sadow's Khar Delba citadel|decoy fortress]] there, with his [[Naga Sadow's Khar Shian citadel|real citadel]] located on Khar Shian,<ref name="fabric" /> of which Sadow was [[Viceroy/Legends|viceroy]].<ref name="EGtW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> In the same [[Standard year/Legends|year]], two [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] explorers from the Republic, [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]], arrived in Sith Space, sending the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] into a frenzy. The two were imprisoned on Ziost, and seeing a opportunity to take the title of Dark Lord of the Sith, Sadow launched an [[First raid on the Sith Citadel|attack]] on their cell at the [[Sith Citadel (Ziost)|Sith Citadel]] and kidnapped them. Sadow brought them to his fortress on Khar Delba while he returned to Ziost, where he proclaimed himself Dark Lord.<ref name="fabric" /> Sadow then split the siblings up, keeping Jori at Khar Delba while sending Gav to his secret fortress on Khar Shian.<ref name="pawns" />

[[File:BattleOfKharDelba3.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Ludo Kressh's forces attack the citadel on Khar Delba.]]
Soon, however, Sadow's rival [[Ludo Kressh]], along with two of his [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] allies, [[Horak-mul]] and [[Dor Gal-ram]], gathered a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] with the intention of [[Battle of Khar Delba|attacking]] Sadow's fortress on Khar Delba. Kressh's force commenced an [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] of Sadow's fortress, but Sadow had a plan. Sadow ordered his [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] [[warrior]]s to man the defense [[Autoturret/Legends|turrets]] of his fortress. While Kressh was distracted with the fortress, Sadow called his secret fleet in from the far side of Khar Shian, which struck at Kressh's flank. In addition, Sadow commanded the subordinates of Horak-mul and Dor Gal-ram, who were actually [[Spy/Legends|double agents]], to betray and [[Death/Legends|murder]] their masters. This tactic worked to perfection, and the two Sith Lords were killed; Kressh found himself with no backup and fled. The Battle of Khar Delba had been won by Sadow. In the midst of the battle, Sadow allowed Jori Daragon to slip away in her [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Starbreaker 12]]'', and return to Republic space. However, Sadow placed a [[Homing beacon/Legends|tracking device]] on the ship's [[Hull/Legends|hull]], which later led Sadow to the Republic to fulfill his expansionist ambitions. On Khar Delba, Sadow slowly rebuilt his fortress, while using the planet as a base to gather his invasion fleet.<ref name="flight" />

In the ensuing [[Standard month/Legends|months]], Sadow put together a [[Sith invasion fleet|fleet]] with which he planned to invade and conquer the Galactic Republic. At a meeting on Khar Delba, where he finally announced that his forces were ready to move out, Sadow received unexpected news; Ludo Kressh had returned to Khar Delba. Kressh's lone [[Ludo Kressh's flagship|flagship]] had arrived in the planet's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], and after Kressh transmitted a message denouncing Sadow, Sadow ordered Gav Daragon, now his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], to activate the controls to block the transmission. Unbeknownst to Daragon, however, Sadow tricked him into pressing a button that destroyed the ship, seemingly killing Kressh. Although Daragon was enraged by this deception, Sadow convinced him it was necessary.<ref name="desperate">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 1]]</ref> The Sith fleet was soon completed and ready for battle, and Sadow set out to conquer the Republic. However, Sadow's Sith were eventually defeated in the ensuing [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], and Sadow was forced to fake his death and flee to the faraway moon of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].<ref name="nec">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

===Later history===
{{Quote|Regarding Seviss Vaa's descriptions of the worlds in Sith space, I can only assume he either lied about his visits to Khar Delba and Khar Shian or that his powers of observation were sorely lacking, as I recovered valuable relics on both worlds.|Palpatine|<ref name="JvS" />}}
[[File:Telosholocron.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Khar Delba was mentioned in the Telos Holocron (pictured).]]
Following the defeat of the Sith Empire, the Sith all but disappeared from Sith Space and the galaxy as a whole. Sith Space was conquered by the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] in the [[Mandalorian Wars]], and later by Dark Lord of the Sith and former [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] in the [[Jedi Civil War]].<ref name="TEA" /> Around [[3638 BBY]],<ref name="Date">{{TOR_updates|2.0}}</ref> the Sith Lord [[Darth Marr]] of the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] departed the planet [[Makeb]] in order to rendevzous with a fleet above Khar Delba, as the Empire was engaged in the [[Galactic War]] with the Republic.<ref name="Rise">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Rise of the Hutt Cartel]]''</ref>

The region around Khar Delba eventually fell into the control of the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]], a Sith group that launched a campaign to control the galaxy, during the [[New Sith Wars]].<ref name="TEA" /> During the New Sith Wars, Brotherhood of Darkness member [[Seviss Vaa]] researched the worlds of the Sith Empire, and mentioned that the world was largely stripped of its Sith treasures by Republic [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] after the end of the Great Hyperspace War in his recordings in the [[Telos Holocron]]. In his addendum, [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine disputed Vaa's claim, as he recovered valuable relics there. The contents of the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] were later published as part of the compendium ''[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force]]'' written by the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Tionne Solusar]] in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]].<ref name="JvS" />

It was left largely untouched by the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], but the area containing Sith Space came to fall into the hands of [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]] by around [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].<ref name="TEA" /> In [[44 ABY]],<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] tasked [[Natua Wan]] with researching information on ancient Sith planets in order to find [[Abeloth]] and a [[Sith Meditation Sphere]] called [[Ship (Sith Meditation Sphere)|Ship]]. Khar Delba was among the planets that [[Ben Skywalker]] read from the [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] and thought were "Top Ten Worst Places to Vacation."<ref name="Ascension">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Ascension]]''</ref> [[137 ABY|More than ninety years later]], the area around Khar Delba fell under the control of [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Inhabitants==
Khar Delba was solely inhabited by Naga Sadow and his servants, many of whom were members of the Sith's Massassi warrior class. Sadow ostensibly resided on Khar Delba exclusively, but secretly held his other fortress on Khar Shian. Jori Daragon also resided on the planet for a short time, living in the guest area of the fortress. The building also contained a large amount of Massassi warriors.<ref name="pawns" /> Although the planet and Sith Space as a whole were originally inhabited by the Sith, they all but disappeared from the area after the events of 5000 BBY.<ref name="TEA" />

==Locations==
A barren, unremarkable planet, Khar Delba contained Naga Sadow's citadel, which served as a decoy for the Sith Lord, to draw attention while Sadow ran his operations from Khar Delba's moon, Khar Shian.<ref name="pawns" /> The citadel was badly damaged due to Ludo Kressh's bombardment of the planet in the Battle of Khar Delba, but Sadow rebuilt it and put together a fleet that went on to attack the Republic in the Great Hyperspace War.<ref name="desperate" />

==Behind the scenes==
The planet Khar Delba first appeared in [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 3|third issue]] of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] miniseries ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|Tales of the Jedi]]: [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith|The Golden Age of the Sith]]'', written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="TotJ:GAotS3">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 3]]</ref> on [[December 11]], [[1996]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54592/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_the_golden_age_of_the_sith_1996_3|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - The Golden Age of the Sith (1996) #3}} {{C|First printing}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the world in grid square R-5.<ref name="TEA" />
